Singer-Loomis validated methodology

Who are you, really?

In just 15 minutes, discover why you've gotten different results on every personality test. We measure all 8 cognitive functions independently—not just four letters.

12,847 assessments taken ~15 minutes No signup required

Your Results Preview

All 8 functions visualized

"Finally, a test that didn't try to box me in."

— Sarah K., Designer

4.9/5 from 847 reviews|132 questions · 8 functions measured|Singer-Loomis validated

You've taken the tests.
You've gotten the letters.
Something still doesn't fit.

You've been typed as an INFJ. Or an ENTP. Or whatever four letters the internet assigned you last Tuesday.

But here's the thing—you've also tested as three other types depending on your mood, the website, or whether you had coffee that morning.

That's not your fault. It's the test's fault.

Most personality assessments force you into 16 predetermined boxes using crude either/or questions. Thinking or Feeling. Sensing or Intuition. As if human psychology works in binary.

It doesn't. Carl Jung knew this. His original work described 8 distinct cognitive functions that exist on a spectrum—present in everyone to varying degrees.

Somewhere along the way, pop psychology threw out the nuance and gave us horoscopes with better marketing.

Why 132 Questions?

Built on Scientific Methodology

This isn't another 10-minute quiz. We use a validated approach that captures the true complexity of your psyche.

Singer-Loomis Methodology

Peer-reviewed since 1980

Unlike MBTI's forced-choice format that assumes you're either Thinking OR Feeling, we measure each of the 8 cognitive functions independently. This reveals your unique profile—not a box you're forced into.

  • Each function scored on its own scale
  • No false dichotomies or forced choices
  • Reveals nuanced function development

Scientific Validation

Grounded in research

132 questions isn't arbitrary. It's the minimum needed to reliably measure 8 distinct cognitive functions with proper statistical validity—something 10-question quizzes simply cannot do.

  • ~16 questions per function for reliability
  • Stress/grip probes for accuracy
  • Based on Jung's original clinical work

Three steps to actual self-understanding

01

Answer 132 questions honestly

No trick questions. No "which animal are you?" nonsense. Each question measures a specific cognitive function based on the Singer-Loomis methodology.

02

See your complete cognitive profile

Your results show how you actually use all 8 functions—not which box you fit into. Visualized as a radar chart unique to you.

03

Understand what it means

Get AI-generated insights about your specific pattern. Not copy-pasted descriptions. Analysis written for your exact results.

12,847 people discovered their type this month

Premium Feature

AI Type Coach in Action

Ask deep questions about your psychology. Get personalized, Jungian-informed insights—not generic advice.

  • Unlimited conversations about your type
  • Shadow work guidance specific to your profile
  • Relationship and career insights

AI Type Coach

Powered by your assessment results

How does my shadow affect my relationships?

Your shadow—rooted in your inferior Extraverted Sensing (

This isn't another MBTI clone

8 Functions Measured Independently

MBTI forces binary choices: you're either a Thinker or a Feeler. We measure all 8 cognitive functions separately, because you use all of them—just in different proportions.

Based on Jung's Actual Work

We built this from Jung's 1921 Psychological Types, not the simplified corporate training version. The Singer-Loomis methodology has been validated in clinical research since the 1980s.

Personalized AI Analysis

No generic "INFJs are creative healers" paragraphs. Our AI writes analysis specific to your function scores—because someone with strong Ti and moderate Fe is different from someone with strong Ti and weak Fe.

Growth-Oriented, Not Just Labeling

Most tests stop at "here's your type." We show you your path to individuation—Jung's term for psychological wholeness. Shadow work prompts, stress patterns, and specific exercises for your profile.

How we compare

Scientific basisWeakMinimalStrongSinger-Loomis validated

"I've taken MBTI tests probably 20 times over the years. Sometimes I'm INFP, sometimes INFJ, once I got ENFP. This assessment showed me why—my Fi and Ni scores are almost identical. The nuance matters."

Questions people ask

Most tests force binary choices—you're either Thinking or Feeling. We measure all 8 cognitive functions on a spectrum, giving you a profile that's actually unique to you. We also base our methodology on Jung's original work and the Singer-Loomis framework, which has clinical validation.

The Singer-Loomis Type Development Inventory, which informs our methodology, has been used in clinical and research settings since the 1980s. Unlike MBTI (which most research psychologists consider unreliable), measuring functions independently produces more consistent, meaningful results.

The free assessment gives you real results—not a teaser. Our Insight tier ($19) unlocks a comprehensive PDF report and full analysis. The Mastery tier ($39) adds the AI Type Coach and growth exercises you won't find elsewhere. Compare that to 16Personalities ($30+) or Truity ($29) which offer less depth.

This isn't about "good" or "bad" types. Every function pattern has strengths and growth areas. The goal is self-understanding, not judgment. That said, if you're unsatisfied with the premium purchase, contact us.

About 15-20 minutes. Answer honestly—don't overthink it.

Not for the free assessment. You'll only need an account if you purchase premium and want to save your results history.

You're 15 minutes away from finally understanding yourself

Join 12,847 others who stopped guessing and started knowing. No signup. No payment. Just answers.

Takes 15 minutes · Results are instant · 100% free

JungianTypology

A tool for self-exploration based on Carl Jung's Psychological Types (1921). Not a diagnostic instrument, but a mirror for reflection.

This assessment relies on self-reporting and cannot access the unconscious directly. Results should be viewed as a "current configuration" rather than a fixed identity.
